initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of first degree AV block?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose first degree AV block, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of flattened T waves in inferior leads
Presence of premature atrial complexes with compensatory pauses
Presence of grouped QRS complexes separated by pauses
Complete dissociation between P waves and QRS complexes

finding_identification.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, looking at the overall rhythm, is every P wave, excluding those following premature beats, consistently followed by a QRS complex on this ECG?

ecg_grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe a non-conducted P wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

diagnostic_decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of first degree AV block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
